title: spergula microsperma
intro: Spergula microsperma, also known as Lesser Sea Spurrey, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism and arthritis, and promoting wound healing. Spergula microsperma can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
